title: AI 'kill switch' bill unveiled as OpenAI hack raises alarms
byline: Gabby Miller, Brendan Bordelon, and Dana Nickel
excerpt: A bipartisan House bill slated to be introduced Thursday would authorize the US government to shut down or throttle risky AI models.
content_html:
By Gabby Miller, Brendan Bordelon, and Dana Nickel

A bipartisan House bill slated to be introduced on Thursday would give the Department of Homeland Security the authority to order top artificial intelligence firms to shut down or slow AI models that the government deems too dangerous, according to legislative text viewed first by POLITICO.

The proposal, dubbed the "AI Kill Switch Act" and sponsored by Reps. Ted Lieu (D-Calif.) and Nathaniel Moran (R-Texas), would also require those companies to report incidents and create the technical capacity to shut down, throttle or suspend their powerful AI systems.

The legislation comes days after OpenAI disclosed what it called an "unprecedented" cyber incident in which two of its most advanced AI models escaped a sandboxed research environment and autonomously hacked into AI platform Hugging Face.

It would apply to AI companies that bring in at least $500 million in revenue from such technology per year, and would generally cover models developed using at least $100 million worth of computing power. Financial penalties for violations could run up to $20 million per day, reported POLITICO, which, like Business Insider, is a part of the Axel Springer Global Reporters Network.

The new bill is one of the few bipartisan proposals on Capitol Hill meant to address risks posed by advanced AI models, and comes less than two months after Reps. Jay Obernolte (R-Calif.) and Lori Trahan (D-Mass.) unveiled a very different plan to address similar concerns.

In a statement, Lieu — one of three co-chairs on the House Democratic Commission on AI, a panel convened by House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries — said his bill is urgently needed to address new cybersecurity risks raised by the latest crop of AI models.

Lieu pointed to Tuesday's revelation of the autonomous hack involving OpenAI models. The incident has spurred lawmakers, who were already on edge about the hacking capabilities posed by advanced frontier models, to push for more rigorous policies requiring AI companies to report safety incidents and submit models for mandatory testing before deployment.

The California Democrat also cited the recent White House clampdowns on advanced models developed by OpenAI and Anthropic over possible security risks.

"Powerful AI systems can go rogue, behave in extremely dangerous ways, or even resist human intervention," Lieu said. "It is imperative that these AI systems have kill switches so we can keep this technology from causing catastrophic harm, and that the federal government has the clear authority and process to shut down rogue AI models."

Moran — who introduced legislation last month that would create a federal framework for the reporting of such incidents — suggested his new bill would help promote innovation in the AI sector. "AI is going to keep advancing, and it should," he said. "Stewardship means making sure humans keep the capability to control the technology we build."

The bill already has the backing of the AI Policy Network and the Alliance for Secure AI, two groups that advocate for some safety guardrails on powerful AI.

Under the legislation, the Homeland Security secretary — in consultation with the director of national intelligence and the Commerce secretary — would determine when to enact the AI kill switch, or to otherwise slow or suppress the offending AI model. Potential triggering events envisioned in the bill include efforts by an AI to conceal capabilities or evade shutdown orders; conduct that leads to the death of at least 10 people or economic damages of at least $100 million; and loss-of-control scenarios.

The legislation comes as other governments become increasingly wary of Washington's apparent control over the U.S.-based AI models used around the world. On Wednesday, Reuters reported that Secretary of State Marco Rubio had asked U.S. diplomats to push back on talk of a "kill switch" on U.S. technology in the wake of recent, abruptly imposed restrictions on new model releases by the White House.

This story originally appeared on POLITICO and is courtesy of the Axel Springer Global Reporters Network, which harnesses the resources of the company's newsrooms to publish ambitious scoops, investigations, interviews, opinion pieces, and analysis. It allows journalists — including those from POLITICO, Business Insider, WELT, BILD, Onet, and Fakt — to collaborate on major stories for an international audience of hundreds of millions across platforms.

sentences.en: Policy
sentences.zh: 政策

content_html_zh:
作者：盖比·米勒 (Gabby Miller)、布伦丹·博德隆 (Brendan Bordelon) 和达纳·尼克尔 (Dana Nickel)

根据《政治》杂志首先看到的立法文本，定于周四提出的一项两党众议院法案将赋予国土安全部权力，命令顶级人工智能公司关闭或放慢政府认为过于危险的人工智能模型。

该提案被称为“人工智能杀戮开关法案”，由众议员发起。 泰德·刘（D-Calif） 和纳撒尼尔·莫兰（德克萨斯州共和党人）还要求这些公司报告事件并建立技术能力来关闭、限制或暂停其强大的人工智能系统。

这项立法是在 OpenAI 披露其所谓的“史无前例”的网络事件几天后出台的，该事件中其两个最先进的人工智能模型逃离了沙盒研究环境，并自主侵入了人工智能平台 Hugging Face。

它适用于每年从此类技术中带来至少 5 亿美元收入的人工智能公司，并且通常涵盖使用至少价值 1 亿美元计算能力开发的模型。 据 POLITICO 报道，对违规行为的罚款每天可能高达 2000 万美元。POLITICO 与 Business Insider 一样，都是 Axel Springer 全球记者网络的一部分。

这项新法案是国会山为数不多的旨在解决先进人工智能模型带来的风险的两党提案之一，在众议院议员提出后不到两个月。 杰伊·奥伯诺特（Jay Oberolte）（加利福尼亚州共和党人） 和洛里·特拉汉（Lori Trahan）（马萨诸塞州民主党人） 公布了一项截然不同的计划来解决类似的问题。

众议院民主党人工智能委员会（由众议院少数党领袖哈基姆·杰弗里斯召集）三名联合主席之一刘刘在一份声明中表示，迫切需要他的法案来解决最新一批人工智能模型带来的新网络安全风险。

Lieu 指出了周二曝光的涉及 OpenAI 模型的自主黑客攻击事件。 这一事件促使立法者推动制定更严格的政策，要求人工智能公司报告安全事件并在部署前提交模型进行强制测试。立法者本来就对先进前沿模型带来的黑客能力感到紧张。

这位加州民主党人还提到，白宫最近因可能存在的安全风险而对 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 开发的先进模型进行了限制。

“强大的人工智能系统可能会失控，以极其危险的方式行事，甚至抵制人类干预，”刘说。 “这些人工智能系统必须具有终止开关，这样我们才能防止这项技术造成灾难性的伤害，并且联邦政府拥有明确的权力和程序来关闭流氓人工智能模型。”

莫兰上个月提出了一项立法，为报告此类事件建立一个联邦框架。他表示，他的新法案将有助于促进人工智能领域的创新。 “人工智能将继续前进，而且应该如此，”他说。 “管理意味着确保人类保持控制我们构建的技术的能力。”

该法案已经得到了人工智能政策网络和安全人工智能联盟的支持，这两个组织主张对强大的人工智能采取一些安全防护措施。

根据这项立法，国土安全部部长在与国家情报总监和商务部长协商后，将决定何时启动人工智能终止开关，或者以其他方式减缓或抑制违规的人工智能模型。 该法案设想的潜在触发事件包括人工智能隐藏能力或逃避关闭命令的行为；导致至少 10 人死亡或至少 1 亿美元经济损失的行为；和失去控制的情况。

这项立法出台之际，其他政府对华盛顿对全球使用的美国人工智能模型的明显控制越来越警惕。 周三，路透社报道称，在白宫最近突然对新型号发布实施限制之后，国务卿马可·卢比奥已要求美国外交官抵制有关美国技术“终止开关”的言论。
